title: Today's Tech Brief: California may let Linux bypass age check
summary: A look at California's potential exemption for Linux from age checks and Ernst & Young's cybersecurity report.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I've got a couple of tech updates for you. First up, California is considering letting Linux bypass age checks. Now, you might wonder what that means. Essentially, some software, like Linux, might not need to go through the same age verification process as other programs. This comes from an exemption in an amendment, which could offer relief to open-source software makers. It's an interesting move, as it shows lawmakers are trying to be flexible with their regulations.

Next, I've got a story about Ernst & Young, the accounting and consulting firm. They published a cybersecurity report on loyalty program safeguards, but here's the kicker: most of the citations in the report were, well, made-up. This was discovered by GPTZero, which investigated the report's claims. It's a bit like finding out your favorite recipe was based on fictional ingredients. You start to wonder what else is missing.

And then there's another story that's been on my mind - the evolving world of tech regulations. With California's potential exemption, it's clear that lawmakers are trying to balance protection with practicality. I mean, who wants to verify their age when using Linux? It's not exactly a social media platform.

California may let Linux bypass age check is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Software California may let Linux bypass age check Exemption in amendment offers relief to open source software makers Thomas Claburn Thomas Claburn Senior reporter Published tue 26 May 2026 // 20:49 UTC The kids are alright. Open source operating systems like Linux and FreeBSD may soon be exempt from California’s app and OS age verification requirements. L... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Ernst & Young published cybersecurity report full of hallucinations is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: GPTZero Investigations · Exclusive Chasing the Hallucinations Ernst & Young (EY) Canada published a cybersecurity report on loyalty program safeguards. We chased down every citation. Most were hallucinated. View Investigation Investigations PE AC Om Ogale , Paul Esau , Alex Cui MAY 14, 2026 Copy link Earlier this year, an engineer at GPTZero coined the term... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
